Project Settings

Configure project identity. This persists between sessions and reports.

Project Information

Max 2MB recommended. Once uploaded, it replaces the default image on the cover page.

Report Period
Use Export/Import to transfer your data when opening a new version of this file.

Task Setup

Define work areas and tasks for this project. On the first report, add them here. Each subsequent month they carry forward automatically.

ℹ️ Tasks and last month's actuals have been carried forward. Last month's actual % is now this month's planned %.
Add New Area
Add Task to Area
Task List

No areas or tasks added yet. Start by adding a work area above.

2.0 Safety Statistics

Enter incident data from Procore. Only incidents recorded this month.

Incident Summary

If filled, this text is used VERBATIM as Section 2.0 in the exported Word document. Leave empty to use the auto-generated intro + numbered incident details. Separate paragraphs with blank lines.

Section 2.0 Preview
Fill in incident counts above to generate preview.

3.1 Planned vs. Actual Work

Enter actual % complete for each task. Add new areas or tasks directly here as needed.

🟢 On track or ahead    🟡 Within 10% of planned    🔴 More than 10% behind — mitigation required
NEW AREA:

No tasks yet.

3.2 Mitigation Strategies

Tasks more than 10% behind are listed below. Describe the cause and mitigation plan for each.

No tasks are currently flagged as behind schedule.

If filled, this text is used VERBATIM as Section 3.2 in the exported Word document. Leave empty to use auto-generated entries from the flagged-task forms above.
Format: each mitigation as ONE paragraph starting with the task name followed by a colon, then the narrative (cause / impact / recovery / verdict).

3.3 Upcoming Work

Enter planned % for next month per task. Add tasks inline under each area. Use "+ Add New Area" only if a brand new area is starting next month.

NEW AREA (if needed):

Add tasks in Step 1 first.

3.4 Schedule Management

Build the schedule management section interactively. The boilerplate text is pre-filled to match the Bockstael template — enter only the project-specific details.

3.4.1 CURRENT SCHEDULE
The current schedule appended to this report includes updates to design and construction activities as of [Set month/year in Settings].
Schedule Milestone Tracking

Yellow Current Schedule cells are what you update each month.

Task Baseline Target (Date) Current Schedule Notes
3.4.2 SCHEDULE PERFORMANCE INDICATOR (SPI)
SPI (Schedule Performance Index) is an indicator of the current progress versus the baseline construction schedule. It is the ratio of Earned Value (actual work complete) to Planned Value (value of work planned at the start-up phase of the project). An SPI of 0.95 - 1.0 means that the project is proceeding as originally scheduled, above 1.0 is ahead of schedule, and below 0.95 means the work is lagging behind.
Preview (this is what will export):
The cumulative SPI for the project is X.X and the project is on schedule for substantial completion on [set Substantial Completion Date in Settings].
3.4.3 CRITICAL PATH VARIANCE
Critical Path Task Breakdown (optional)

If you want to list the tasks currently on the critical path, add them below. The export will emit "Tasks on the critical path include:" followed by area bullets, each with their tasks as indented sub-bullets. Leave empty to omit entirely.

Section 4.0 — Project Issues / Risk Management

Capture project issues and the risk heat map context. Use the per-field Copilot help to draft narratives in the Bockstael voice.

4.1 PROJECT ISSUES

List each material project issue this period. Provide a short title (for internal tracking) and a narrative paragraph that will be exported into the report.

4.2 PROJECT RISK HEAT MAP
Risk Counts (this period)
Preview (this is what will export as the first paragraph of 4.2):
[Set risk counts and month to see preview]
Risk Heat Map Image (browser preview only)

Optional — for your own reference while editing. The export always emits a bold + yellow-highlighted placeholder line — "Insert screenshot image of Project Risk Heat Map here." — at the end of section 4.2; paste the actual image into the .docx at that marker after generation.

Key Risks (Currently "Red")

List risks that are flagged "Red" this period. Each becomes a bullet in the export. Leave empty if none.

Section 5.0 — Submittal Management

Permits, RFIs, and submittals. Permit info carries forward month to month; RFI list and submittal counts update monthly.

5.1 PERMITS

List each permit/approval relevant to the project. Each row exports as a bulleted line. Information carries forward month over month — edit/remove as permits get issued.

5.2 REQUESTS FOR INFORMATION

Enter this month's RFI totals and the outstanding RFI list. The export uses the project month from Settings.

If left blank, falls back to RFI list count (0)
Preview (this is what will export):
There were X RFIs submitted in [month], X of them has been closed. There is currently 0 outstanding RFIs.
Outstanding RFI List

Each row exports as a numbered line with a color-coded priority badge. Red=HIGH, Orange=MEDIUM, Green=LOW.

5.3 SUBMITTALS
Boilerplate intro exported automatically: "Submittals include shop drawings, product data, samples, sample warranties, testing/inspections, mock-ups, and certificates from the manufacturer."
Submittal Status Counts

Enter the count for each status. Colors match the template export.

Status Count

Section 6.0 — Change Management

6.1 Change Order Log and 6.2 Change in Process are auto-emitted as boilerplate — fill in the cells in Word post-export. 6.3 and 6.4 are interactive here.

6.1 CURRENT CHANGE ORDER LOG

Enter the count, value, and additional days for each status category. The Total Changes row auto-sums all three. Status row colors (pink/yellow/green) and bold Total row apply automatically in the export.

Total Value Add'l Days
6.2 CHANGE IN PROCESS
Auto-emitted on export. The exported .docx will contain a yellow-highlighted placeholder line — "[PM: List CCNs in process here as bulleted items.]" — that you replace with actual CCN bullets in Word post-export.
6.3 ANTICIPATED CHANGES

List items where change documentation is anticipated. The export auto-emits the intro line "Change documentation is anticipated for the following items:" followed by your bullets.

6.4 STATUS OF CASH ALLOWANCES

4-column table. Description and Contract Amount carry forward month over month. Consumed To Date (highlighted in yellow) updates monthly. Balance Remaining auto-calculates as Contract − Consumed. A bold TOTAL row at the bottom sums each numeric column.

Description Contract Amount Consumed To Date Balance (auto)
6.5 STATUS OF CONTINGENCY (optional)

Contingency table columns vary by project, so the actual table is entered in Word post-export. Check the box below to include section 6.5 in the export — the .docx will get the 6.5 heading + a yellow-highlighted placeholder marker for you to paste your project-specific contingency table.

Section 7.0 — Financial Summary

Auto-generated from Settings page values (contract value + monthly billings). Update Settings if values change. The cash flow table is pasted in Word post-export.

7.0 INTRO PARAGRAPH (AUTO-GENERATED)

Update Total Contract Value and Monthly Billings on the Settings page. The period-ending date auto-computes from Settings month/year.

Preview (this exports as the first paragraph of 7.0):
[Set month/year and contract/billing values in Settings to see preview]
7.1 CASH FLOW ACTUAL AND REMAINING FORECAST

7.1 auto-emits these two lines:

Insert screenshot image of cash flow table here.
Please note that the forecast shown above does not include pending Changes to Contract.

The yellow line is a placeholder — paste your actual cash flow table screenshot at that marker in the Word doc post-export.

Section 8.0 — Quality Assurance Documents

Two auto-generated intros (this month + next month, both from Settings). Add one quality item per row — each row exports as its own bullet. (Round 60)

8.0 — COMPLETED THIS MONTH
Auto-generated intro:
[Set month in Settings to see preview]
8.0 — UPCOMING NEXT MONTH
Auto-generated intro:
[Set month in Settings to see preview]

If both lists are empty, the export falls back to the yellow-highlighted placeholder so the PM/PC can fill it in Word later. When you add items here, each row exports as its own bullet — clean, no yellow highlight, no post-export editing required.

Section 9.0 — Photographs

Always starts on a new page. Auto-emits a single yellow-highlighted placeholder bullet for you to replace in Word with captioned progress photos.

9.0 PLACEHOLDER (AUTO-EMITTED)

Section 9.0 always starts on a fresh page. The export emits the heading + one yellow-highlighted placeholder bullet line below. Replace it in Word with your captioned progress photos.

Insert Photos

The yellow-highlighted "Insert Photos" line is impossible to miss in the exported docx. Replace it with your captioned progress photos before sending.

Generate & Export

Review AI-generated report sections. Edit if needed, then export to Word.
